answer.text: Market forces of supply and demand among option buyers and sellers — NOT fixed by exchanges or SEBI
The clearing corporation based on VaR calculations

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Option prices are discovered through market trading — buyers and sellers with different views on volatility and price direction arrive at premiums. Exchanges only provide the platform; SEBI ensures smooth functioning. **The Trap:** Neither SEBI nor exchanges fix option prices. The clearing corporation sets margins, not option premiums. Price discovery is fundamental to all derivative markets. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers the same principle for equity option pricing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Implied volatility drives option premiums — when market uncertainty rises, implied volatility increases and premiums spike even if the underlying price hasn't moved.
